author: Tim McGraw
content: I have three daughters, so I can't be as tough as I want to be. When you have kids - especially daughters - they know how to work you. They're a lot smarter than we are, that's for sure. But I'll be more tough on their boyfriends.

author: Meryl Streep
content: The interesting thing about being a mother is that everyone wants pets, but no one but me cleans the kitty litter.

author: Matthew Broderick
content: Having a baby changes the way you view your in-laws. I love it when they come to visit now. They can hold the baby and I can go out.

author: Roseanne Barr
content: I figure that if the children are alive when I get home, I've done my job.

author: Rita Rudner
content: I want to have children, but my friends scare me. One of my friends told me she was in labor for 36 hours. I don't even want to do anything that feels good for 36 hours.

author: David Frost
content: Love is staying up all night with a sick child - or a healthy adult.

author: Bob Filner
content: Childhood obesity is best tackled at home through improved parental involvement, increased physical exercise, better diet and restraint from eating.

author: Christy Turlington
content: Parents should not smoke in order to discourage their kids from smoking. A child is more likely to smoke when they have been raised in the environment of a smoker.

author: Hodding Carter
content: There are only two lasting bequests we can hope to give our children. One of these is roots, the other, wings.

author: John Boehner
content: Studies show that children best flourish when one mom and one dad are there to raise them.

author: Patricia Heaton
content: It's hard enough to work and raise a family when your kids are all healthy and relatively normal, but when you add on some kind of disability or disease, it can just be such a burden.

author: Franklin P. Jones
content: You can learn many things from children. How much patience you have, for instance.

author: Miguel de Cervantes
content: No fathers or mothers think their own children ugly.

author: Connie Sellecca
content: You have to support your children to have a healthy relationship.

author: Benjamin Spock
content: The child supplies the power but the parents have to do the steering.

author: Bruce Jenner
content: If you're asking your kids to exercise, then you better do it, too. Practice what you preach.

author: Cindy Crawford
content: It's a huge change for your body. You don't even want to look in the mirror after you've had a baby, because your stomach is just hanging there like a Shar-Pei.

author: Abigail Van Buren
content: It is a sad commentary of our times when our young must seek advice and counsel from 'Dear Abby' instead of going to Mom and Dad.

author: Alvin Toffler
content: Parenthood remains the greatest single preserve of the amateur.

author: Benjamin Spock
content: What good mothers and fathers instinctively feel like doing for their babies is usually best after all.

author: Clive Owen
content: Parenthood and family come first for me, and when I'm not working I'm cool with the Teletubbies.

author: Kelly LeBrock
content: Success for me its to raise happy, healthy human beings.

author: Kamisese Mara
content: The family teaches us about the importance of knowledge, education, hard work and effort. It teaches us about enjoying ourselves, having fun, keeping fit and healthy.

author: Marion Jones
content: My father wasn't really involved and my mom is the light in my life.

author: Liza Minnelli
content: I've said it before, but it's absolutely true: My mother gave me my drive, but my father gave me my dreams. Thanks to him, I could see a future.

author: Allan Bloom
content: Fathers and mothers have lost the idea that the highest aspiration they might have for their children is for them to be wise... specialized competence and success are all that they can imagine.

author: Robert Cormier
content: Family life was wonderful. The streets were bleak. The playgrounds were bleak. But home was always warm. My mother and father had a great relationship. I always felt 'safe' there.

author: Ogden Nash
content: Parents were invented to make children happy by giving them something to ignore.

author: Alan Thicke
content: Family involvement is a valuable thing and playing together actively can be the '90s version of it. Instead of just watching, you can do it together... something we don't spend enough time on. We can motivate and excite each other about fitness.

author: Milton Sapirstein
content: Education, like neurosis, begins at home.

author: Reese Witherspoon
content: I feel very blessed to have two wonderful, healthy children who keep me completely grounded, sane and throw up on my shoes just before I go to an awards show just so I know to keep it real.

author: Marcelene Cox
content: No one knows his true character until he has run out of gas, purchased something on the installment plan and raised an adolescent.

author: Beverly Cleary
content: I don't think children's inner feelings have changed. They still want a mother and father in the very same house they want places to play.

author: Andrew Shue
content: There are 80 million moms in the United States. Forty million stay at home with their children.

author: Norma Shearer
content: I get whatever placidity I have from my father. But my mother taught me how to take it on the chin.

author: Anne Sullivan
content: It's a great mistake, I think, to put children off with falsehoods and nonsense, when their growing powers of observation and discrimination excite in them a desire to know about things.

author: Tina Turner
content: I regret not having had more time with my kids when they were growing up.

author: Marjorie Holmes
content: What feeling is so nice as a child's hand in yours? So small, so soft and warm, like a kitten huddling in the shelter of your clasp.

author: John Sculley
content: We expect teachers to handle teenage pregnancy, substance abuse, and the failings of the family. Then we expect them to educate our children.

author: Brooke Shields
content: I think I'm going to have to live vicariously through my daughter's rebellion because I certainly never did go through adolescence.

author: Stevie Nicks
content: Do you want to be an artist and a writer, or a wife and a lover? With kids, your focus changes. I don't want to go to PTA meetings.

author: Robbie Coltrane
content: Believe me, my children have more stamina than a power station.

author: Kenny G
content: That's my ideal day, time with my boys.

author: Lynn Johnston
content: Mom and Dad would stay in bed on Sunday morning, but the kids would have to go to church.

author: Ellen Goodman
content: We criticize mothers for closeness. We criticize fathers for distance. How many of us have expected less from our fathers and appreciated what they gave us more? How many of us always let them off the hook?

author: Ryan Phillippe
content: Home life's great, man. The kids are great, happy and healthy. I've reached this sort of wonderful precipice.

author: John H. Johnson
content: My mother was the influence in my life. She was strong she had great faith in the ultimate triumph of justice and hard work. She believed passionately in education.

author: John Walters
content: We all have an interest in making sure teens grow up healthy and drug-free.

author: June Allyson
content: I'd just as soon stay home and raise babies.

author: Coretta Scott King
content: Mama and Daddy King represent the best in manhood and womanhood, the best in a marriage, the kind of people we are trying to become.

author: Isabelle Huppert
content: My mom's a Catholic, and my dad's a Jew, and they didn't want anything to do with anything.

author: Marie Osmond
content: There are some great questions to ask your doctor. If he says 'no,' then you find yourself a different doctor. There really has to be a change in how we medically look at women at this time. I mean, this is not just baby gloom.

author: Jill Clayburgh
content: I wasn't very good about juggling family and my career. I was interested in who was coming to the children's birthday party, what my son was writing. I was thinking about Legos.

author: Roberto Benigni
content: My father was a farmer and my mother was a farmer, but, my childhood was very good. I am very grateful for my childhood, because it was full of gladness and good humanity.

author: Bill Cosby
content: No matter how calmly you try to referee, parenting will eventually produce bizarre behavior, and I'm not talking about the kids. Their behavior is always normal.

author: Bill Cosby
content: My childhood should have taught me lessons for my own fatherhood, but it didn't because parenting can only be learned by people who have no children.

author: Ben Stein
content: Screaming at children over their grades, especially to the point of the child's tears, is child abuse, pure and simple. It's not funny and it's not good parenting. It is a crushing, scarring, disastrous experience for the child. It isn't the least bit funny.

author: Virginia Satir
content: What lingers from the parent's individual past, unresolved or incomplete, often becomes part of her or his irrational parenting.

author: Temple Grandin
content: Autism is a neurological disorder. It's not caused by bad parenting. It's caused by, you know, abnormal development in the brain. The emotional circuits in the brain are abnormal. And there also are differences in the white matter, which is the brain's computer cables that hook up the different brain departments.

author: Jennifer Aniston
content: Parenting is one of the hardest jobs on earth.

author: Barbara Bush
content: I'm worried about parents who aren't parenting.

author: Jennifer Aniston
content: Of course, the ideal scenario for parenting is obviously two parents of a mature age.

author: Louise Hart
content: The Golden Rule of Parenting is do unto your children as you wish your parents had done unto you!

author: Audre Lorde
content: I would like to do another piece of fiction dealing with a number of issues: Lesbian parenting, the 1960's, and interracial relationships in the Lesbian and Gay community.

author: Dennis Miller
content: Parenting is the most important job on the planet next to keeping Gary Busey off the nation's highways.

author: Emma Thompson
content: Indeed - judicious, consistent parenting is a dream of mine. No judgements, learning space and listening carefully are my goals.

author: Tori Spelling
content: I thought that once we were out of the baby stage, parenting would be a breeze.

author: Jonathan Safran Foer
content: Kids are a great analogy. You want your kids to grow up, and you don't want your kids to grow up. You want your kids to become independent of you, but it's also a parent's worst nightmare: That they won't need you. It's like the real tragedy of parenting.

author: Mary J. Blige
content: I barely have time for my own children. To adopt more children and not have time for them, that would be poor parenting on my part.

author: Clive Owen
content: Very often when you see families it's all perfect and neat, and parenting isn't like that. You do have constant negotiations. Things are ever developing and ever changing, and you constantly have to evaluate how you deal with your kids.

author: Edie Falco
content: We're living in a time when parenting is not at all mirroring the way I was parented. For me, I just followed my parents around on their errands when they were busy on the phone, I was quiet. It's a different kettle of fish these days: They run the house, and you listen to their music, and you go to their appointments.

author: Eliot Spitzer
content: Don't reward bad behavior. It is one of the first rules of parenting. During the financial cataclysm of 2008, we said it differently. When we bailed out banks that had created their own misfortune, we called it a 'moral hazard,' because the bailout absolved the bank's bad acts and created an incentive for it to make the same bad loans again.

author: Erica Jong
content: Women are individuals in parenting, and why not?

author: Jane Fonda
content: While not impossible, it is especially challenging for teenage parents to develop bonds with their children. A high percent of them were themselves children of teenage parents and have never experienced appropriate parenting.

author: Fiona Apple
content: No, I've never wanted kids. But I do read about parenting a lot.

author: Eddie Vedder
content: I'm trying to break any chain of negative parenting that I might have survived.

author: Sloane Crosley
content: My grandmother was a kind of Scarsdale, New York, society woman, best known in her day as the author of the 1959 book 'Growing Your Own Way: An Informal Guide for Teen-Agers' - this despite being a person whose parenting style made Joan Crawford's wire hangers look like pool noodles.

author: Sloane Crosley
content: Ah, the power of two. There's nothing quite like it. Especially when it comes to paying utility bills, parenting, cooking elaborate meals, purchasing a grown-up bed, jumping rope and lifting heavy machinery. The world favours pairs. Who wants to waste the wood building an ark for singletons?

author: Debra Winger
content: I hope to find the roles that are age appropriate but not yearning to be younger, or parenting ad nauseam.

author: Nancy Chodorow
content: Parenting, as an unpaid occupation outside the world of public power, entails lower status, less power, and less control of resources than paid work.

author: Harrison Ford
content: Parenting is an impossible job at any age.

author: Fred G. Gosman
content: We must return optimism to our parenting. To focus on the joys, not the hassles the love, not the disappointments the common sense, not the complexities.

author: Padma Lakshmi
content: In truth, I am a single mother. But I don't feel alone at all in parenting my daughter. Krishna has a whole other side of her family who loves her, too. And so Krishna is parented by me, but also by her grandmother and aunts and cousins and uncles and friends.

author: Mayim Bialik
content: Relationships are complicated no matter what style of parenting you choose.

author: Mayim Bialik
content: Attachment parenting is not a passive parenting style.

author: Mayim Bialik
content: I've become sort of an accidental advocate for attachment parenting, which is a style of parenting that... basically, the way mammals parent and the way people have parented for pretty much all of human history except the last 200 years or so.

author: Mayim Bialik
content: I have a neuroscience background - that's what my doctorate is in - and I was trained to study hormones of attachment, so I definitely feel my parenting is informed by that.

author: Mayim Bialik
content: I came to parenting the way most of us do - knowing nothing and trying to learn everything.

author: Mika Brzezinski
content: I realize that of all people, I am no expert on parenting or marriage.

author: Robert Casey
content: Abortion is defended today as a means of ensuring the equality and independence of women, and as a solution to the problems of single parenting, child abuse, and the feminization of poverty.

author: Uma Thurman
content: So, you know, parenting is a very intimate and amazing experience and one of the best experiences of my life.

author: Joan Lunden
content: I had three children while doing a show, as demanding as 'Good Morning America,' so this is - you know, it's almost like I'm less daunted about motherhood, and parenting at this point in time. And I think I'm just much more fit and healthy than I was 20-years-ago.

author: Bob Barr
content: For decades, parents were told by so-called parenting 'experts' that offspring would be best raised on the belief each is special and entitled to all life has to offer.

author: Jillian Michaels
content: Parenting is not for sissies. You have to sacrifice and grow up.

author: Henry Reed
content: If there is any truth to my parenting the dreamwork movement, it comes from the power of the press.

author: Ryan Phillippe
content: I grew up with no money. My kids will grow up with a lot of money and so it's really important to me, and it will always be a part of my parenting, to keep them conscientious and connected socially to other people.

author: Joan Cusack
content: I take parenting incredibly seriously. I want to be there for my kids and help them navigate the world, and develop skills, emotional intelligence, to enjoy life, and I'm lucky to be able to do that and have two healthy, normal boys.

author: Andrew Bernstein
content: The number of stressors has multiplied exponentially: traffic, money, success, work/life balance, the economy, the environment, parenting, family conflict, relationships, disease. As the nature of human life has become far more complicated, our ancient stress response hasn't been able to keep up.

author: Eric McCormack
content: I'm torn about late parenting. I believe people should spend their twenties living and having fun and not having any regrets later. I also think people in their thirties generally make better parents but so many of my friends are having trouble - myself included - as fathers get older.

author: Robbie Coltraine
content: My kids love it. I thought I was the coolest dad in the world when I got to be in a Bond film, but 'Harry Potter', too? Well, I think I qualify for a medal for exceptional parenting or something, don't you?

author: Mel Carnahan
content: Parents are key when it comes to keeping kids off drugs. Good parenting is the best anti-drug we have.

author: Hector Elizondo
content: I'm a parent, especially when you've had the intense parenting the way I had. It's all in the bank. It's all in the great experience bank. Those are your secrets. That's the stuff that makes your work rich, that's what you dip into.

author: Michael Showalter
content: For me, Twitter works best as a way of taking pictures of being stuck in traffic on the Brooklyn Bridge. If people really want to read really funny quips about life, parenting, and pop culture, then by all means read Michael Ian Black's tweets.

author: Arlie Russell Hochschild
content: In response to our fast-food culture, a 'slow food' movement appeared. Out of hurried parenthood, a move toward slow parenting could be growing. With vital government supports for state-of-the-art public child care and paid parental leave, maybe we would be ready to try slow love and marriage.

author: Lisa Whelchel
content: I'm hosting weekend retreats all over America. It is like a 24-hour slumber party for moms. We laugh, eat, play games, get massages, win prizes, talk about parenting and even cry a bit.

author: Carl Jung
content: Even a happy life cannot be without a measure of darkness, and the word happy would lose its meaning if it were not balanced by sadness. It is far better take things as they come along with patience and equanimity.

author: Robert H. Schuller
content: Never cut a tree down in the wintertime. Never make a negative decision in the low time. Never make your most important decisions when you are in your worst moods. Wait. Be patient. The storm will pass. The spring will come.

author: Napoleon Hill
content: Patience, persistence and perspiration make an unbeatable combination for success.

author: Helen Keller
content: We could never learn to be brave and patient, if there were only joy in the world.

author: William Shakespeare
content: How poor are they that have not patience! What wound did ever heal but by degrees?

author: Joseph Addison
content: Our real blessings often appear to us in the shape of pains, losses and disappointments but let us have patience and we soon shall see them in their proper figures.

author: Jean-Jacques Rousseau
content: Patience is bitter, but its fruit is sweet.

author: John Burroughs
content: For anything worth having one must pay the price and the price is always work, patience, love, self-sacrifice - no paper currency, no promises to pay, but the gold of real service.

author: Thomas Fuller
content: Abused patience turns to fury.

author: Elbert Hubbard
content: How many a man has thrown up his hands at a time when a little more effort, a little more patience would have achieved success.

author: Phillips Brooks
content: Be patient and understanding. Life is too short to be vengeful or malicious.

author: Saint Francis de Sales
content: Have patience with all things, But, first of all with yourself.

author: George Savile
content: A man who is a master of patience is master of everything else.

author: Solomon Ibn Gabirol
content: The test of good manners is to be patient with the bad ones.

author: Edith Sitwell
content: I am patient with stupidity but not with those who are proud of it.

author: Julius Caesar
content: It is easier to find men who will volunteer to die, than to find those who are willing to endure pain with patience.

author: Saadi
content: Have patience. All things are difficult before they become easy.

author: Edmund Burke
content: Our patience will achieve more than our force.

author: Michelangelo
content: Genius is eternal patience.

author: Heraclitus
content: Good character is not formed in a week or a month. It is created little by little, day by day. Protracted and patient effort is needed to develop good character.

author: Margaret Thatcher
content: I'm extraordinarily patient provided I get my own way in the end.

author: Thomas Carlyle
content: Endurance is patience concentrated.

author: Soren Kierkegaard
content: Patience is necessary, and one cannot reap immediately where one has sown.

author: Arnold H. Glasow
content: The key to everything is patience. You get the chicken by hatching the egg, not by smashing it.

author: Ambrose Bierce
content: Patience, n. A minor form of dispair, disguised as a virtue.

author: Jesse Jackson
content: I am not a perfect servant. I am a public servant doing my best against the odds. As I develop and serve, be patient. God is not finished with me yet.

author: John Ruskin
content: Endurance is nobler than strength, and patience than beauty.

author: George Jackson
content: Patience has its limits. Take it too far, and it's cowardice.

author: Simone Weil
content: Humility is attentive patience.

author: Francis Bacon
content: Who ever is out of patience is out of possession of their soul.

author: Rudyard Kipling
content: Heaven grant us patience with a man in love.

author: Paul Kane
content: As for goals, I don't set myself those anymore. I'm not one of these 'I must have achieved this and that by next year' kind of writers. I take things as they come and find that patience and persistence tend to win out in the end.

author: Thomas Fuller
content: All commend patience, but none can endure to suffer.

author: William Penn
content: Patience and Diligence, like faith, remove mountains.

author: Luc de Clapiers
content: Patience is the art of hoping.

author: Sivananda
content: There is something good in all seeming failures. You are not to see that now. Time will reveal it. Be patient.

author: Lawrence G. Lovasik
content: The practice of patience toward one another, the overlooking of one another's defects, and the bearing of one another's burdens is the most elementary condition of all human and social activity in the family, in the professions, and in society.

author: Kin Hubbard
content: Lack of pep is often mistaken for patience.

author: Charles Caleb Colton
content: Patience is the support of weakness impatience the ruin of strength.

author: Plautus
content: Patience is the best remedy for every trouble.

author: Isaac Newton
content: If I have done the public any service, it is due to my patient thought.

author: Thomas a Kempis
content: All men commend patience, although few are willing to practice it.

author: Hal Borland
content: If you would know strength and patience, welcome the company of trees.

author: Laurence J. Peter
content: Fortune knocks but once, but misfortune has much more patience.

author: Ovid
content: Bear patiently with a rival.

author: John Dryden
content: Beware the fury of a patient man.

author: Baltasar Gracian
content: Let him that hath no power of patience retire within himself, though even there he will have to put up with himself.

author: Lord Chesterfield
content: Patience is the most necessary quality for business, many a man would rather you heard his story than grant his request.

author: Willie Aames
content: God gives me the children's ministry heart and patience. This is what He wants. It's awesome. I don't know where He's gonna take it - but God is building this thing.

author: Tertullian
content: Hope is patience with the lamp lit.

author: Jean de la Bruyere
content: There is no road too long to the man who advances deliberately and without undue haste there are no honors too distant to the man who prepares himself for them with patience.

author: John Updike
content: A healthy male adult bore consumes each year one and a half times his own weight in other people's patience.

author: William Golding
content: He who rides the sea of the Nile must have sails woven of patience.

author: Thomas Hardy
content: Patience, that blending of moral courage with physical timidity.

author: Hyman Rickover
content: Good ideas are not adopted automatically. They must be driven into practice with courageous patience.

author: Phaedrus
content: Everyone ought to bear patiently the results of his own conduct.

author: Stanislaw Lec
content: You must first have a lot of patience to learn to have patience.

author: Bret Harte
content: Never a tear bedims the eye that time and patience will not dry.

author: Philip Massinger
content: Patience, the beggar's virtue, shall find no harbor here.

author: Moliere
content: A wise man is superior to any insults which can be put upon him, and the best reply to unseemly behavior is patience and moderation.

author: Harriet Tubman
content: Every great dream begins with a dreamer. Always remember, you have within you the strength, the patience, and the passion to reach for the stars to change the world.

author: Ralph Marston
content: The keys to patience are acceptance and faith. Accept things as they are, and look realistically at the world around you. Have faith in yourself and in the direction you have chosen.

author: Ralph Waldo Emerson
content: Adopt the pace of nature: her secret is patience.

author: Mahatma Gandhi
content: If patience is worth anything, it must endure to the end of time. And a living faith will last in the midst of the blackest storm.

author: Lao Tzu
content: I have just three things to teach: simplicity, patience, compassion. These three are your greatest treasures.

author: Jean de La Fontaine
content: Patience and time do more than strength or passion.

author: George Washington
content: The marvel of all history is the patience with which men and women submit to burdens unnecessarily laid upon them by their governments.

author: Benjamin Franklin
content: He that can have patience can have what he will.

author: William Shakespeare
content: No, I will be the pattern of all patience I will say nothing.

author: Victor Hugo
content: Have courage for the great sorrows of life and patience for the small ones and when you have laboriously accomplished your daily task, go to sleep in peace.

author: Francis Quarles
content: Beware of him that is slow to anger for when it is long coming, it is the stronger when it comes, and the longer kept. Abused patience turns to fury.

author: Saint Augustine
content: Patience is the companion of wisdom.

author: Elizabeth Taylor
content: It is strange that the years teach us patience that the shorter our time, the greater our capacity for waiting.

author: Leo Tolstoy
content: The two most powerful warriors are patience and time.

author: Anthony Hamilton
content: I learned patience, perseverance, and dedication. Now I really know myself, and I know my voice. It's a voice of pain and victory.

author: Charlie Chaplin
content: I do not have much patience with a thing of beauty that must be explained to be understood. If it does need additional interpretation by someone other than the creator, then I question whether it has fulfilled its purpose.

author: John Quincy Adams
content: Patience and perseverance have a magical effect before which difficulties disappear and obstacles vanish.

author: Sammy Davis, Jr.
content: Alcohol gives you infinite patience for stupidity.

author: Michelle Obama
content: And I love that even in the toughest moments, when we're all sweating it - when we're worried that the bill won't pass, and it seems like all is lost - Barack never lets himself get distracted by the chatter and the noise. Just like his grandmother, he just keeps getting up and moving forward... with patience and wisdom, and courage and grace.

author: Corazon Aquino
content: Faith is not simply a patience that passively suffers until the storm is past. Rather, it is a spirit that bears things - with resignations, yes, but above all, with blazing, serene hope.

author: Ambrose Bierce
content: Meekness: Uncommon patience in planning a revenge that is worth while.

author: Elizabeth Edwards
content: The days of our lives, for all of us, are numbered. We know that. And yes, there are certainly times when we aren't able to muster as much strength and patience as we would like. It's called being human.

author: Anne Morrow Lindbergh
content: I do not believe that sheer suffering teaches. If suffering alone taught, all the world would be wise, since everyone suffers. To suffering must be added mourning, understanding, patience, love, openness and the willingness to remain vulnerable.

author: Helen Hayes
content: When traveling with someone, take large does of patience and tolerance with your morning coffee.

author: Barbara Johnson
content: Patience is the ability to idle your motor when you feel like stripping your gears.

author: Francois de La Rochefoucauld
content: We have no patience with other people's vanity because it is offensive to our own.

author: Chuck Tanner
content: There are three secrets to managing. The first secret is have patience. The second is be patient. And the third most important secret is patience.

author: Thomas Aquinas
content: To bear with patience wrongs done to oneself is a mark of perfection, but to bear with patience wrongs done to someone else is a mark of imperfection and even of actual sin.

author: Gertrude Jekyll
content: A garden is a grand teacher. It teaches patience and careful watchfulness it teaches industry and thrift above all it teaches entire trust.

author: James Russell Lowell
content: Endurance is the crowning quality, And patience all the passion of great hearts.

author: George Santayana
content: It takes patience to appreciate domestic bliss volatile spirits prefer unhappiness.

author: Hal Borland
content: Knowing trees, I understand the meaning of patience. Knowing grass, I can appreciate persistence.

author: May Sarton
content: Everything that slows us down and forces patience, everything that sets us back into the slow circles of nature, is a help. Gardening is an instrument of grace.

author: Thomas Huxley
content: Patience and tenacity are worth more than twice their weight of cleverness.

author: David Hobson
content: I grow plants for many reasons: to please my eye or to please my soul, to challenge the elements or to challenge my patience, for novelty or for nostalgia, but mostly for the joy in seeing them grow.

author: Charles Stanley
content: The Scriptures contain many stories of people who waited years or even decades before the Lord's promises came to pass. What modern believers can learn from the patience of biblical saints like Abraham, Joseph, David, and Paul is that waiting upon the Lord has eternal rewards.

author: Alice Walker
content: Clearly older women and especially older women who have led an active life or elder women who successfully maneuver through their own family life have so much to teach us about sharing, patience, and wisdom.

author: Ellen DeGeneres
content: I'd like to be more patient! I just want everything now. I've tried to meditate, but it's really hard for me to stay still. I'd like to try to force myself to do it, because everybody says how wonderful meditation is for you, but I can't shut my mind up. So patience and learning is the key.

author: Henry Rollins
content: I have not the smarts or patience for political office.

author: David Mallet
content: Affliction is the wholesome soil of virtue, where patience, honor, sweet humility, and calm fortitude, take root and strongly flourish.

author: George W. Bush
content: I think you can judge from somebody's actions a kind of a stability and sense of purpose perhaps created by strong religious roots. I mean, there's a certain patience, a certain discipline, I think, that religion helps you achieve.

author: Cleveland Amory
content: As anyone who has ever been around a cat for any length of time well knows, cats have enormous patience with the limitations of the human kind.

author: Guy Kawasaki
content: Patience is the art of concealing your impatience.

author: Holbrook Jackson
content: Patience has its limits, take it too far and it's cowardice.

author: Daisaku Ikeda
content: With love and patience, nothing is impossible.

author: Elisha Cuthbert
content: When I was 20, I thought I was 30 - but I was so far from it. When you're young, you want everything to happen now. As you mature, you can look back and see all the great things you achieved with time and patience.

author: Franklin P. Jones
content: You can learn many things from children. How much patience you have, for instance.

author: Peter Marshall
content: Teach us, O Lord, the disciplines of patience, for to wait is often harder than to work.

author: Gertrude Stein
content: I have declared that patience is never more than patient. I too have declared, that I who am not patient am patient.

author: Khaleda Zia
content: It is impossible to practice parliamentary politics without having patience, decency, politeness and courtesy.

author: Jerry Garcia
content: I have all the patience in the world about Sirens. For me it's not a Grateful Dead project, it's a Me project.

author: George MacDonald
content: The principle part of faith is patience.

author: Margaret J. Wheatley
content: Hopelessness has surprised me with patience.

author: Ryunosuke Satoro
content: Find patience in the breath of life.

author: E. Joseph Cossman
content: The greatest power is often simple patience.

author: Paul Sweeney
content: How can a society that exists on instant mashed potatoes, packaged cake mixes, frozen dinners, and instant cameras teach patience to its young?

author: Ralph Fiennes
content: Gardeners are good at nurturing, and they have a great quality of patience, they're tender. They have to be persistent.

author: John Major
content: The first requirement of politics is not intellect or stamina but patience. Politics is a very long run game and the tortoise will usually beat the hare.

author: David Duchovny
content: I think patience is a skill and I wish I had it.

author: Eknath Easwaran
content: Patience can't be acquired overnight. It is just like building up a muscle. Every day you need to work on it.

author: Abu Bakr
content: There is no harm in patience, and no profit in lamentation. Death is easier to bear (than) that which precedes it, and more severe than that which comes after it. Remember the death of the Apostle of God, and your sorrow will be lessened.

author: Cesar Chavez
content: There is no substitute for hard work, 23 or 24 hours a day. And there is no substitute for patience and acceptance.

author: Robert Falcon Scott
content: I can imagine few things more trying to the patience than the long wasted days of waiting.

author: Katherine Heigl
content: I'm terrible with patience.

author: Debra Winger
content: If I could have one prayer answered, I would pray for patience. I move so fast sometimes. I try to slow down.

author: David Wilkerson
content: A final word: I am not knowledgeable about the internet. I do not have a computer. I guess that at 74 years of age, I don't have the patience to learn.

author: Julie Bowen
content: It's a war of attrition. If you have patience and a modicum of faith in yourself your chances are not too bad.

author: Aaron Spelling
content: A show that no one thought had a chance has just finished its fifth year: Charmed. I think it's tougher for the younger networks, so I think they have a little more patience for the sake of the show. But who knows?

author: Dick Dale
content: I don't play pyrotechnic scales. I play about frustration, patience, anger. Music is an extension of my soul.

author: James J. Corbett
content: Only those who have patience to do simple things perfectly ever acquire the skill to do difficult things easily.

author: Larry Hagman
content: There are very little things in this life I cannot afford and patience is one of them.

author: Bryan Cranston
content: Luck is a component that a lot of people in the arts sometimes fail to recognise: that you can have talent, perseverance, patience, but without luck you will not have a successful career.

author: Stanley Baldwin
content: A statesman wants courage and a statesman wants vision but believe me, after six months' experience, he wants first, second, third and all the time - patience.

author: Vanessa Paradis
content: My mum told me to have patience. It's about realising that when things aren't going the way you want them to, or you don't have inspiration, it will come.

author: Hart Crane
content: It has taken a great deal of energy, which has not been so difficult to summon as the necessary patience to wait, simply wait much of the time - until my instincts assured me that I had assembled my materials in proper order for a final welding into their natural form.

author: Martin Yan
content: So when I do Chinese cooking, I mix everything together, then the kids have to eat their vegetables. They won't have the patience to pick them out.

author: Bob Woodward
content: I believe there's too little patience and context to many of the investigations I read or see on television.

author: Tony Kushner
content: Artists know that diligence counts as much, if not more, as inspiration in art, as in politics, patience counts as much as revolution.

author: Damian Lewis
content: Temperamentally I'm not a natural producer, because I don't have the patience.

author: Gary Vaynerchuk
content: You have to respect your parents. They are giving you an at-bat. If you're an entrepreneur and go into the family business, you want to grow fast. Patience is important. But respect the other party... My dad and I pulled it off because we really respect each other.

author: Evelyn Underhill
content: Deliberately seek opportunities for kindness, sympathy, and patience.

author: Melissa Rosenberg
content: Everyone is given one gift, a reason for being, and it's our obligation to do something with it. Obviously, it's a challenge - but if you're not taking the bull by the horns, I have no patience for you. You're just taking up space.

author: Muammar al-Gaddafi
content: We are telling the American people to have patience, courage, resolve and determination.

author: T. Boone Pickens
content: I think I have more patience now than I did in the past.

author: Michael Haneke
content: Because I'm the author of my screenplays I know what I'm looking for. It's true that I can be stubborn in demanding that I get what I want, but it's also a question of working with patience and love.

author: Lauren Bacall
content: Patience was not my strong point.

author: Lexa Doig
content: Thank God my parents had an abundance of patience.

author: Carl Hiaasen
content: One problem with age is that patience begins to ebb.

author: Paul Gleason
content: My biggest weakness is patience, wanting to see things happen too quickly or get changes in place right away. Not having the patience to let things develop.

author: Lincoln Diaz-Balart
content: I haven't been given many things, but I've been given ample, limitless patience.

author: Jim Bishop
content: When you read about a car crash in which two or three youngsters are killed, do you pause to dwell on the amount of love and treasure and patience parents poured into bodies no longer suitable for open caskets?

author: Gail Simmons
content: Patience was not something that came naturally to me, but in cooking it is the quintessential skill.

author: Gail Simmons
content: Patience is the secret to good food.

author: Gail Simmons
content: You know, I lose patience really easily I'd rather shop in the grocery store than in the department store. I can pick an apple like nobody's business.

author: Gerry Adams
content: Republican patience with how unionism deals with the political institutions, and with key issues like equality and human rights, will be tested because, obviously, there will be a battle a day on these matters. So lets face up to all of this with our eyes wide open.

author: Judith Light
content: You have to find the peace and patience within yourself to be a model and an example to others and not judge.

author: Richard Cecil
content: God's way of answering the Christian's prayer for more patience, experience, hope and love often is to put him into the furnace of affliction.

author: Star Jones
content: No, you're not allowed to be bossy when you're married. You have to learn compromise, and compassion and patience.

author: Ernie Banks
content: People ask me a lot about the values I got from playing for the Cubs for so many years. The value I got out of it was patience. A lot of people these days are not very patient.

author: Kenny Loggins
content: I've worked so hard to eliminate the inner geek from my life. I suddenly realize I have no patience for those people who still have their geeks showing. Now I see why being 'normal' has been so important to me.

author: Andrew Bird
content: Honestly, I didn't have the patience for biology or history in an academic sense, but I always liked the kind of big questions.

author: Brett Ratner
content: In this day and age, you need a lot of patience if you are in the movie business.

author: Beverly Cleary
content: When I was in the first grade I was afraid of the teacher and had a miserable time in the reading circle, a difficulty that was overcome by the loving patience of my second grade teacher. Even though I could read, I refused to do so.

author: Ingrid Newkirk
content: Give your dog or cat respect, patience, understanding and love. And if you just change to one vegetarian day a week, that's a wonderful step that will save animal lives. It means you have chosen something kind instead of something cruel.

author: Wesley Morris
content: In movies, there are some things the French do that Americans are increasingly incapable of doing. One is honoring the complexities of youth. It's a quiet, difficult undertaking, requiring subtlety in a filmmaker and perception and patience from us.

author: Alan Sugar
content: I have no patience at all.

author: Herman Hesse
content: Writing is good, thinking is better. Cleverness is good, patience is better.

author: Tokugawa Ieyasu
content: The strong manly ones in life are those who understand the meaning of the word patience.

author: Janet Napolitano
content: And we ask the American people to play an important part of our layered defense. We ask for cooperation, patience and a commitment to vigilance in the face of a determined enemy.

author: Ismail Haniyeh
content: When I said a few weeks ago that our people would eat cooking oil and olives if necessary, I didn't mean that there really would be only oil and olives. What I meant was that our people have the necessary patience to endure the current difficult situation. Palestinians would rather do without certain food items than their national rights.

author: Zoe Kazan
content: I don't have a lot of patience for boring arthouse movies.

author: Ryan White
content: We had great faith that with patience, understanding, and education, that my family and I could be helpful in changing their minds and attitudes around.

author: Elihu Root
content: Secretary of War Stanton used to get out of patience with Lincoln because he was all the time pardoning men who ought to be shot.

author: Christina Milian
content: My advice to someone to follow in my footsteps is to have patience. I've been doing this for twelve years.

author: Charles Sturt
content: Now it is evident that a little insight into the customs of every people is necessary to insure a kindly communication this, joined with patience and kindness, will seldom fail with the natives of the interior.

author: Matthew Arnold
content: Sad Patience, too near neighbour to despair.

author: Randy Johnson
content: Work hard. And have patience. Because no matter who you are, you're going to get hurt in your career and you have to be patient to get through the injuries.

author: Andrew Greeley
content: Is the patience of the American people that long suffering? Is there no outrage left in the country?

author: Darrell Issa
content: I've said my patience is not infinite.

author: Leon Jouhaux
content: True enough, nature has endowed me with a fair measure of patience and composure, yet I should be lying if I told you that, having seen the reporter off on his way to make his deadline, I fell peacefully asleep.

author: Frances O'Grady
content: My impression is that most women public service workers have a long fuse. Precisely because they care so deeply about services, more than anyone, they still want to find a sensible and fair negotiated agreement. But their patience has run out.

author: Dweezil Zappa
content: While it is true that Frank had a great sense of humor, he was also very serious about composing music. In reality there are only a handful of skilled players who can play his most complex pieces. It takes a lot of patience to learn and requires a fantastic memory.

author: Niki Taylor
content: I think God's really blessed me with patience. I'm good with a lot of things going on at once.

author: Lyman Abbott
content: Patience is passion tamed.

author: Milla Jovovich
content: I'm not really easy to live with! There needs to be unlimited patience and unconditional love. Men I've known before loved my independent spirit and were proud of my success, to the point that they'd become jealous of the time I devote to my career.

author: Jenny Agutter
content: I have a huge respect for writers and realise that this is not an area that I find easy. I doubt that I would have the patience in front of a blank sheet of paper to become a writer.

author: Gustavo Dudamel
content: My relationship with the Philharmonia Orchestra brought me many times to London and I will always reflect positively on that early period of development with them - their patience, their warmth, their dedication.

author: Tom Holt
content: Everything is out there if you know how to find it, and have the patience. I don't and haven't, but that's my problem.

author: Thomas Perry
content: Reading a novel in which all characters illustrate patience, hard work, chastity, and delayed gratification could be a pretty dull experience.

author: Major Taylor
content: I pray they will carry on in spite of that dreadful monster prejudice, and with patience, courage, fortitude and perseverance achieve success for themselves.

author: Frank Rich
content: Americans have less and less patience for the intrusive and divisive moral scolds who thrived in the bubbles of the Clinton and Bush years.

author: Harry Shum, Jr.
content: I've always been a person with patience, and I don't like to force things.

author: Pauline Kael
content: It seems likely that many of the young who don't wait for others to call them artists, but simply announce that they are, don't have the patience to make art.

author: Cee Lo Green
content: Tolerance, compromise, understanding, acceptance, patience - I want those all to be very sharp tools in my shed.

author: Tokugawa Ieyasu
content: Patience means restraining one's inclinations.

author: Tom Lantos
content: The patience of the American public with dilatory diplomatic delays will be very limited.

author: Dave Navarro
content: I wish I was a shredder that could play everything, but I don't have the patience.

author: Jeremy Sisto
content: There's not a lot of room anymore for what I call 'made-up' drama. The drama comes from real places now - marriage takes work and focus, the kid stuff takes patience and commitment. And if you don't grow as people and as a couple, within all of that, then you've got some real drama.

author: Elliot Paul
content: Patience makes a women beautiful in middle age.

author: Paul Bremer
content: I think the Iraqi people have shown extraordinary patience and courage in the last few months. They have really put a political system on the way to success, to a real democracy here.

author: Jack Ma
content: The very important thing you should have is patience.

author: Giorgio Napolitano
content: Speaking from my experience as a person involved for a long time in building the European Union, it is important to have patience and efforts to build a community of nations.

author: Wil Wheaton
content: My wife is the most awesome person in the universe. She's made this experience much less miserable for me, with her compassion, patience and understanding.

author: Michael Chiklis
content: Patience has never been my strong suit.

author: Nicholas Brendon
content: Constant repetition of tongue-twisters was like lifting weights for me, but patience and persistence have paid off.

author: Peter Stuyvesant
content: Your patience would fail you if I should continue to relate all the disrespectful speeches and treatment which your servants have been obliged to listen to and patiently to bear.

author: George Tenet
content: To understand a difficult topic like Iraq takes patience and care. Unfortunately, you rarely hear a patient, careful or thoughtful discussion of intelligence these days.

author: Randy Travis
content: I've learned the importance of loving what you do. I have also learned more patience due to the nature of the music business.

author: Jeffrey Katzenberg
content: I haven't got a lot of patience.

author: Candace Cameron Bure
content: It's amazing. My kids have grown me in ways I never knew possible. The patience I've received and the love I get from them is just amazing.

author: Joshua Slocum
content: Anyhow, a philosophical turn of thought now was not amiss, else one's patience would have given out almost at the harbour entrance. The term of her probation was eight days.

author: Charles Churchill
content: Patience is sorrow's salve.

author: Adam Scott
content: You know, writing is really difficult, and it takes a real patience and a skill. I don't know if I have that. I admire it in others, so much, and I envy it.

author: Paulina Porizkova
content: Beauty, unlike the rest of the gifts handed out at birth, does not require dedication, patience and hard work to pay off. But it's also the only gift that does not keep on giving.

author: Robert Wise
content: My three Ps: passion, patience, perseverance. You have to do this if you've got to be a filmmaker.

author: Bill Brandt
content: Sometimes they are a matter of luck the photographer could not expect or hope for them. Sometimes they are a matter of patience, waiting for an effect to be repeated that he has seen and lost or for one that he anticipates.

author: Kathleen Wynne
content: I think there's not much patience for organized labour, period, public or private sector.

author: Ray Harryhausen
content: That's why I never became a director. I never had patience with people.

author: John Florio
content: Patience is the best medicine.

author: Simon Raven
content: And so, at the age of thirty, I had successively disgraced myself with three fine institutions, each of which had made me free of its full and rich resources, had trained me with skill and patience, and had shown me nothing but forbearance and charity when I failed in trust.

author: H. G. Bohn
content: He preacheth patience that never knew pain.

author: Bernard de Mandeville
content: The only thing of weight that can be said against modern honor is that it is directly opposite to religion. The one bids you bear injuries with patience, the other tells you if you don't resent them, you are not fit to live.

author: Yingluck Shinawatra
content: I will exercise patience and will provide all facts to the general public.

author: Thomas Edward Brown
content: As I pass it, I feel as if I saw a dear old mother, sweet in her weakness, trembling at the approach of her dissolution, but not appealing to me against the inevitable, rather endeavouring to reassure me by her patience, and pointing to a hopeful future.

author: Wilkie Collins
content: This is the story of what a Woman's patience can endure, and what a Man's resolution can achieve.

author: Abdus Salam
content: Soon I knew the craft of experimental physics was beyond me - it was the sublime quality of patience - patience in accumulating data, patience with recalcitrant equipment - which I sadly lacked.

author: Ridley Pearson
content: As a young boy, I read 'Cheaper by the Dozen' and immediately became neurotic about my use of time. It taxed me severely, but only for the next 50 years. But I think it also allowed me to discipline myself to sit in the chair and be a writer, where one of the most needed qualities is patience.

author: Yiannis Stournaras
content: Difficult years lie ahead, patience is required.

author: Ilie Nastase
content: If you have confidence you have patience. Confidence, that is everything.

author: Bowie Kuhn
content: So it's been a slow process and it's taken some patience. That's why patients are called patients I think - patience is required.

author: Tyrone Power
content: I've done an awful lot of stuff that's a monument to public patience.

author: Ronald Reagan
content: If we ever forget that we are One Nation Under God, then we will be a nation gone under.

author: Albert Einstein
content: Nationalism is an infantile disease. It is the measles of mankind.

author: John F. Kennedy
content: Let every nation know, whether it wishes us well or ill, that we shall pay any price, bear any burden, meet any hardship, support any friend, oppose any foe to assure the survival and the success of liberty.

author: Mark Twain
content: Patriot: the person who can holler the loudest without knowing what he is hollering about.

author: John F. Kennedy
content: I look forward to a great future for America - a future in which our country will match its military strength with our moral restraint, its wealth with our wisdom, its power with our purpose.

author: Oscar Wilde
content: Patriotism is the virtue of the vicious.

author: Malcolm X
content: You're not supposed to be so blind with patriotism that you can't face reality. Wrong is wrong, no matter who says it.

author: George Bernard Shaw
content: Patriotism is your conviction that this country is superior to all others because you were born in it.

author: Voltaire
content: It is lamentable, that to be a good patriot one must become the enemy of the rest of mankind.

author: Barack Obama
content: We, the People, recognize that we have responsibilities as well as rights that our destinies are bound together that a freedom which only asks what's in it for me, a freedom without a commitment to others, a freedom without love or charity or duty or patriotism, is unworthy of our founding ideals, and those who died in their defense.

author: Clarence Darrow
content: True patriotism hates injustice in its own land more than anywhere else.

author: Aldous Huxley
content: One of the great attractions of patriotism - it fulfills our worst wishes. In the person of our nation we are able, vicariously, to bully and cheat. Bully and cheat, what's more, with a feeling that we are profoundly virtuous.

author: Dale Carnegie
content: Each nation feels superior to other nations. That breeds patriotism - and wars.

author: Blaise Pascal
content: Can anything be stupider than that a man has the right to kill me because he lives on the other side of a river and his ruler has a quarrel with mine, though I have not quarrelled with him?

author: George McGovern
content: The highest patriotism is not a blind acceptance of official policy, but a love of one's country deep enough to call her to a higher plain.

author: Calvin Coolidge
content: Patriotism is easy to understand in America. It means looking out for yourself by looking out for your country.

author: Douglas MacArthur
content: Our government has kept us in a perpetual state of fear - kept us in a continuous stampede of patriotic fervor - with the cry of grave national emergency.

author: Bertrand Russell
content: Patriotism is the willingness to kill and be killed for trivial reasons.

author: George Orwell
content: Patriotism is usually stronger than class hatred, and always stronger than internationalism.

author: Samuel Johnson
content: Patriotism is the last refuge of the scoundrel.

author: Howard Zinn
content: Dissent is the highest form of patriotism.

author: Barbara Ehrenreich
content: No matter that patriotism is too often the refuge of scoundrels. Dissent, rebellion, and all-around hell-raising remain the true duty of patriots.

author: Charles de Gaulle
content: Patriotism is when love of your own people comes first nationalism, when hate for people other than your own comes first.

author: Bill Vaughan
content: A real patriot is the fellow who gets a parking ticket and rejoices that the system works.

author: Leo Tolstoy
content: The greater the state, the more wrong and cruel its patriotism, and the greater is the sum of suffering upon which its power is founded.

author: Eugene V. Debs
content: I have no country to fight for my country is the earth, and I am a citizen of the world.

author: William Ralph Inge
content: A nation is a society united by a delusion about its ancestry and by common hatred of its neighbours.

author: George Jean Nathan
content: Patriotism is often an arbitrary veneration of real estate above principles.

author: Doc Hastings
content: 137 years later, Memorial Day remains one of America's most cherished patriotic observances. The spirit of this day has not changed - it remains a day to honor those who died defending our freedom and democracy.

author: Guy de Maupassant
content: Patriotism is a kind of religion it is the egg from which wars are hatched.

author: Diogenes
content: I am not an Athenian or a Greek, but a citizen of the world.

author: George Santayana
content: To me, it seems a dreadful indignity to have a soul controlled by geography.

author: Dave Barry
content: We'll try to cooperate fully with the IRS, because, as citizens, we feel a strong patriotic duty not to go to jail.

author: Ann Coulter
content: Liberals become indignant when you question their patriotism, but simultaneously work overtime to give terrorists a cushion for the next attack and laugh at dumb Americans who love their country and hate the enemy.

author: Edith Cavell
content: I realize that patriotism is not enough. I must have no hatred or bitterness towards anyone.

author: Francis Ford Coppola
content: Most Italians who came to this country are very patriotic. There was this exciting possibility that if you worked real hard, and you loved something, you could become successful.

author: Bill Maher
content: I do think the patriotic thing to do is to critique my country. How else do you make a country better but by pointing out its flaws?

author: Alfred Adler
content: It is the patriotic duty of every man to lie for his country.

author: Lyn Nofziger
content: The tree of liberty needs to be watered from time to time with the blood of patriots and tyrants.

author: Mick Jagger
content: Patriotism is an instant reaction that fades away when the war starts.

author: Clara Barton
content: The patriot blood of my father was warm in my veins.

author: David Hume
content: The heights of popularity and patriotism are still the beaten road to power and tyranny.

author: Jane Addams
content: Unless our conception of patriotism is progressive, it cannot hope to embody the real affection and the real interest of the nation.

author: Ho Chi Minh
content: It was patriotism, not communism, that inspired me.

author: Arthur C. Clarke
content: It is not easy to see how the more extreme forms of nationalism can long survive when men have seen the Earth in its true perspective as a single small globe against the stars.

author: Sinclair Lewis
content: Intellectually I know that America is no better than any other country emotionally I know she is better than every other country.

author: Henry A. Wallace
content: They are patriotic in time of war because it is to their interest to be so, but in time of peace they follow power and the dollar wherever they may lead.

author: Jesse Ventura
content: Patriotism is voluntary. It is a feeling of loyalty and allegiance that is the result of knowledge and belief. A patriot shows their their patriotism through their actions, by their choice.

author: George Galloway
content: I'm an advocate of the great Dr. Johnson, the English man of letters who said that patriotism was the last refuge of the scoundrel.

author: William Shenstone
content: The proper means of increasing the love we bear our native country is to reside some time in a foreign one.

author: Earl Warren
content: The most tragic paradox of our time is to be found in the failure of nation-states to recognize the imperatives of internationalism.

author: Friedrich Durrenmatt
content: We do not consider patriotism desirable if it contradicts civilized behavior.

author: Pablo Casals
content: The love of one's country is a splendid thing. But why should love stop at the border?

author: Michael Ignatieff
content: I distinguish, between nationalism and patriotism.

author: Robert Walpole
content: The very idea of true patriotism is lost, and the term has been prostituted to the very worst of purposes. A patriot, sir! Why, patriots spring up like mushrooms!

author: John F. Kerry
content: I saw courage both in the Vietnam War and in the struggle to stop it. I learned that patriotism includes protest, not just military service.

author: Charles Edward Montague
content: Patriotism has served, at different times, as widely different ends as a razor, which ought to be used in keeping your face clean and yet may be used to cut your own throat or that of an innocent person.

author: Richard Aldington
content: Nationalism is a silly cock crowing on his own dunghill.

author: Henry Bolingbroke
content: Patriotism must be founded on great principals and supported by great virtue.

author: George Borrow
content: Next to the love of God, the love of country is the best preventive of crime.

author: Mark Twain
content: Patriotism is supporting your country all the time, and your government when it deserves it.

author: Albert Einstein
content: Heroism on command, senseless violence, and all the loathsome nonsense that goes by the name of patriotism - how passionately I hate them!

author: George Washington
content: Guard against the impostures of pretended patriotism.

author: George Bernard Shaw
content: You'll never have a quiet world till you knock the patriotism out of the human race.

author: Bob Dylan
content: I define nothing. Not beauty, not patriotism. I take each thing as it is, without prior rules about what it should be.

author: Ambrose Bierce
content: What this country needs what every country needs occasionally is a good hard bloody war to revive the vice of patriotism on which its existence as a nation depends.

author: Ambrose Bierce
content: Patriotism. Combustible rubbish ready to the torch of any one ambitious to illuminate his name.

author: George William Curtis
content: A man's country is not a certain area of land, of mountains, rivers, and woods, but it is a principle and patriotism is loyalty to that principle.

author: Richard M. Nixon
content: Americans admire a people who can scratch a desert and produce a garden. The Israelis have shown qualities that Americans identify with: guts, patriotism, idealism, a passion for freedom. I have seen it. I know. I believe that.

author: Malcom X
content: You're not supposed to be so blind with patriotism that you can't face reality. Wrong is wrong no matter who does it or who says it.

author: Nick Lampson
content: But this Veterans Day, I believe we should do more than sing the praises of the bravery and patriotism that our veterans have embodied in the past. We should take this opportunity to re-evaluate how we are treating our veterans in the present.

author: Arthur Schopenhauer
content: Patriotism, when it wants to make itself felt in the domain of learning, is a dirty fellow who should be thrown out of doors.

author: O. Henry
content: Love and business and family and religion and art and patriotism are nothing but shadows of words when a man's starving!

author: Ricardo Montalban
content: Politics is too partisan, and sometimes patriotism is cast aside. Patriotism is honor and love of your country and your brothers and sisters. With politics I get the impression that it's all about what's good for the party and not necessarily what's good for the country.

author: Ted Cruz
content: Look, I think Hispanic community - the values that resonate in our community are fundamentally conservative. They are faith, family and patriotism.

author: Jimmy Carter
content: Unfortunately, after Sept. 11, there was an outburst in America of intense suffering and patriotism, and the Bush administration was very shrewd and effective in painting anyone who disagreed with the policies as unpatriotic or even traitorous.

author: Scott Ritter
content: One, if you attack my integrity, I will defend myself. If you attack my patriotism, I will defend myself. If you come after my family, I will counter-attack viciously, I will destroy you.

author: Howard Thurman
content: During times of war, hatred becomes quite respectable even though it has to masquerade often under the guise of patriotism.

author: Denis Diderot
content: Patriotism is an ephemeral motive that scarcely ever outlasts the particular threat to society that aroused it.

author: Henry Louis Gates
content: Patriotism is best exemplified through auto-critique. When you're willing to stand up within the group and say, 'It is wrong for Black people to be anti-Semitic,' or 'It is wrong for America to discriminate against persons of African descent and made them slaves and based its wealth upon free labor,' it's crucial to say that.

author: Henry Louis Gates
content: Patriotism is best exemplified through auto-critique.

author: Jesse Ventura
